---
title: "Homework 9"
format: html
---

**Due:** Friday, April 17, 11:59pm on Canvas

**Instructions:** For mathematical work, you may choose to either hand-write your work and submit a PDF scan, or type your work using LaTeX and submit the resulting PDF. For computational work, include all code and R output necessary to answer the questions, and submit as an html or pdf on Canvas.

See the course website for a [homework template](https://sta711-s26.github.io/homework/hw_template.tex) file and [instructions](https://sta711-s26.github.io/homework/latex_instructions/) on getting started with LaTeX and Overleaf.


## Sign test

Suppose we have iid data $X_1,...,X_n$ from some symmetric distribution $f(x|\theta)$ with mean and median $\theta$, and finite variance $\sigma^2$. We wish to test the hypotheses
$$H_0: \theta = 0 \hspace{1cm} H_A: \theta > 0$$

We know that the **Wald test** rejects when $\dfrac{\overline{X}_n - 0}{s/\sqrt{n}} > z_{\alpha} = \Phi^{-1}(1 - \alpha)$.

The Wald test is testing a hypothesis about the *mean* of the distribution. What if we instead wanted to test a hypothesis about the *median*? For a symmetric distribution, the mean and median will be the same, so we are doing inference on the same parameter -- but as we know from previous homework, sometimes it is more efficient to estimate the median than the mean.

Let $T(\boldsymbol{X}) = \frac{1}{n} \sum \limits_{i=1}^n \mathbb{I}\{X_i > 0\}$. The **sign test** of the hypotheses $H_0: \theta = 0$ vs. $H_A: \theta > 0$ rejects when $T(\boldsymbol{X}) > t_0$, for some cutoff $t_0$.

The purpose of this section of the assignment is to compare the sign test and the Wald test.
